generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance BuildingHVACActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical actuator for HVAC systems configured by Building Control Engineers within BIM environment, enabling remote operation by Facility Managers via the Digital Twin framework. Includes metadata extraction for addressing mechanisms (tenant/device IDs, endpoint addresses)."
]

instance BuildingLightingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical actuator for lighting systems configured by Building Control Engineers within BIM environment, supporting remote control operations through the Digital Twin framework with safety-validated synchronization."
]

instance BuildingValveActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical valve actuator configured in BIM models by Building Control Engineers, enabling real-time control via Digital Twin with automated metadata extraction for operational addressing."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance GenericBCSSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Generic Building Control System sensor configured by Building Control Engineers in BIM environment, supporting real-time data collection for Digital Twin synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData BCSRealTimeTelemetry
]

instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Temperature sensor integrated into BIM models by Building Control Engineers, providing real-time environmental data for Digital Twin monitoring and control."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalTemperatureData
]

instance HumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Humidity sensor configured within BIM environment by Building Control Engineers, enabling real-time data collection for Digital Twin environmental monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalHumidityData
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Occupancy sensor defined in BIM models by Building Control Engineers, providing real-time occupancy metrics for Digital Twin operational awareness."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyMetrics
]

instance StructuralHealthSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Structural health monitoring sensor configured in BIM environment, enabling real-time data collection for Digital Twin predictive maintenance services."
    DTDFVocab:producedData StructuralIntegrityData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance BCSRealTimeTelemetry : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time telemetry data from Building Control System sensors, transmitted via Eclipse Hono for protocol-agnostic ingestion into the Digital Twin framework."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GenericBCSSensor
]

instance EnvironmentalTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time temperature data collected from sensors, synchronized bidirectionally between physical building and Digital Twin via Eclipse Ditto."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance EnvironmentalHumidityData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time humidity data transmitted from physical sensors to Digital Twin for environmental monitoring and control optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HumiditySensor
]

instance OccupancyMetrics : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time occupancy data collected from sensors, enabling Digital Twin services for space utilization optimization and energy management."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance StructuralIntegrityData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time structural health data transmitted from physical sensors to Digital Twin for predictive maintenance and integrity validation."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom StructuralHealthSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance EnrichedBIMModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Building Information Model enriched with Building Control System components (sensors, actuators, controllers) by Building Control Engineers, serving as the foundational static representation for Digital Twin deployment."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BIMToDTTransformationEnabler
]

instance DeviceModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Structured device model extracted from enriched BIM, representing physical sensors/actuators with semantic metadata for Digital Twin integration."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DeviceModelProcessorEnabler
]

instance ControlLogicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Control logic model derived from BIM configurations, defining operational workflows and validation rules for Digital Twin actuators."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlLogicValidatorEnabler
]

instance OperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time operational data including sensor readings, actuator states, and environmental parameters synchronized bidirectionally between physical building and Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:fromData BCSRealTimeTelemetry, EnvironmentalTemperatureData, EnvironmentalHumidityData, OccupancyMetrics, StructuralIntegrityData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RealTimeDataProcessorEnabler
]

instance HistoricalPerformanceData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Historical performance data collected over building lifecycle, used for trend analysis, predictive maintenance, and long-term optimization in Digital Twin services."
    DTDFVocab:fromData BCSRealTimeTelemetry
    DTDFVocab:inputTo HistoricalDataAnalyzerEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance BIMToDTTransformationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Driven Engineering enabler that transforms enriched BIM models into deployable Digital Twin artifacts, automating semantic extraction and runtime configuration generation."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance DeviceModelProcessorEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler for processing device models extracted from BIM, establishing addressing mechanisms and synchronization protocols for Digital Twin components."
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimizationService, DecisionSupportService
]

instance ControlLogicValidatorEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler for validating and refining control logic defined in BIM models, ensuring consistency between physical and digital representations."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, DecisionSupportService
]

instance RealTimeDataProcessorEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler for processing real-time data from Eclipse Hono and Ditto, ensuring continuous synchronization between physical sensors and Digital Twin representations."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance HistoricalDataAnalyzerEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler for analyzing historical performance data, supporting predictive maintenance and long-term optimization services in the Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveAnalyticsService, OptimizationService
]

instance EclipseHonoIngestionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Hono-based enabler for secure, protocol-agnostic IoT data ingestion from heterogeneous sensors into the Digital Twin framework."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance EclipseDittoSynchronizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Ditto-based enabler for event-driven data synchronization and state management between physical building and Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, DecisionSupportService
]

instance UnrealEngineVisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Unreal Engine-based enabler for immersive 3D/4D visualization, supporting VR/AR integration and dynamic scenario simulations in the Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationAndCollaborationService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service aggregating and visualizing runtime data (sensor telemetry, occupancy metrics, environmental conditions) for continuous operational awareness and anomaly detection."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalStateInsight, AnomalyDetectionAction
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service leveraging data-driven simulations to forecast system degradation, maintenance needs, or energy consumption trends, reducing unplanned downtime."
    DTDFVocab:provides DegradationForecastInsight, MaintenanceRecommendationAction
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service applying algorithmic optimization for energy efficiency, space utilization, or structural integrity across building lifecycle phases."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyEfficiencyRecommendationInsight, SpaceUtilizationOptimizationAction
]

instance DecisionSupportService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service delivering enhanced decision-making capabilities by integrating simulation outcomes, predictive insights, and real-time data into stakeholder workflows."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlLogicValidationInsight, ResourceAllocationRecommendationAction
]

instance VisualizationAndCollaborationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing immersive, multi-dimensional representations (3D BIM models with real-time overlays) for stakeholder collaboration across building lifecycle."
    DTDFVocab:provides ImmersiveVisualizationInsight, CollaborativeDecisionMakingAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance OperationalStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing Facility Managers with real-time visualization and analysis of system states (sensor data, actuator status) via interactive tools."
]

instance AnomalyDetectionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic action triggered by detected anomalies in real-time monitoring data, alerting operators to potential issues."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DegradationForecastInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight forecasting system degradation based on predictive analytics, enabling proactive maintenance planning."
]

instance MaintenanceRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action providing Building Control Engineers with recommended maintenance schedules based on predictive analytics."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EnergyEfficiencyRecommendationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight recommending energy efficiency improvements derived from optimization algorithms applied to real-time and historical data."
]

instance SpaceUtilizationOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action proposing space utilization optimizations based on occupancy metrics and predictive analytics."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ControlLogicValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight validating control logic defined in BIM models against real-time operational data for consistency verification."
]

instance ResourceAllocationRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action recommending optimal resource allocation based on predictive insights and current operational state."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ImmersiveVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing Building Control Engineers and Facility Managers with 3D/4D immersive representations of building operations."
]

instance CollaborativeDecisionMakingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action facilitating multi-stakeholder decision-making through shared immersive visualization and real-time data integration."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C1: Multiplicities (Digital Twin Instances)
instance FacilityManagerDTInstance : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Single-instance Digital Twin focused on real-time monitoring and control of deployed physical assets, tailored for Facility Managers."
]

instance ExtendedDTInstance : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Extended Digital Twin instance created by DT Engineers through service integration (e.g., adding analytics or third-party tools)."
]

instance EnrichedBIMDerivedDTInstance : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Enriched BIM-derived Digital Twin representing the static-to-dynamic transition for Building Control Engineers during deployment."
]

instance DTFrameworkHub : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Centralized Digital Twin Framework acting as a hub for bidirectional data exchange and runtime configuration management across heterogeneous instances."
]

instance DTEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains FacilityManagerDTInstance, ExtendedDTInstance, EnrichedBIMDerivedDTInstance, DTFrameworkHub
    base:desc "Modular and hierarchical Digital Twin environment supporting role-specific instances with centralized coordination via the DT Framework."
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual_to_physical_interaction** in this DT system involves **command-driven control flows** from the digital twin to physical building systems, governed by the following technical specifications:
  - **Command Types**: Operational directives (e.g., HVAC adjustments, lighting control, equipment activation) derived from real-time monitoring and analytics.
  - **Control Message Formats**: Standardized formats ensuring interoperability with physical systems, though exact syntax (e.g., JSON, XML, proprietary) is not specified.
  - **Validation and Authentication Mechanisms**: Implicitly required to ensure secure and authorized command execution (referenced in **NF_X** for interoperability and maintainability).
  - **Feedback Systems**: Bidirectional confirmation loops where physical systems acknowledge command receipt/execution, enabling closed-loop control (e.g., status updates, error signals).
  - **Conflict Resolution**: Not explicitly detailed, but the **runtime configuration management** capability suggests dynamic adjustments to resolve conflicts without full redeployment.
  - **Command Execution Confirmation**: Real-time validation of command outcomes, leveraging the DT’s **interactive visualization** for immersive monitoring by *Facility Managers* (as per Figure 1)."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system emphasizes **real-time data exchange capabilities** to ensure **continuous synchronization** between physical buildings and their digital counterparts, enabling **live synchronization** of operational states. The framework supports **real-time monitoring and control** through bidirectional data flows, though specific numerical latency requirements (e.g., millisecond-level or sub-100ms thresholds) are not defined in the provided documents. The temporal granularity is implied to be **high-resolution**, aligning with the dynamic operational needs of the AECO (Architecture, Engineering, Construction, and Operations) domain, as opposed to static BIM representations. The system leverages **Eclipse Hono and Ditto** as the underlying integration layer for real-time data exchange, ensuring low-latency communication between physical assets and their digital twins. No explicit time scales for service-specific synchronization (e.g., control loops vs. analytics) are specified, but the framework’s design prioritizes **temporal fidelity** for operational decision-making."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs a **coordinated, role-based architecture pattern** centered on the **DT Framework**, which orchestrates interactions between physical buildings, BIM models, and operational services. Key components include:
  - **Enriched BIM Models** (static input) transformed into **operational DTs** via a **life-cycle-aware deployment pipeline**,
  - **Bidirectional Data Exchange** between physical assets and digital counterparts, facilitated by the framework’s **central hub**,
  - **Modular Service Integration** via standardized extension points, enabling dynamic addition of analytics or third-party tools,
  - **Runtime Configuration Management** for adaptive resource allocation and system-wide adjustments without redeployment.
  The constellation leverages **interoperability standards** (implied by NF_X requirements) to ensure seamless coordination across heterogeneous systems, while **scalability mechanisms** (from components to entire infrastructure) manage resource constraints. The system-wide coordination is further reinforced by **low-overhead maintainability** and **accessibility** for users with varying technical expertise."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the proposed model-driven deployment framework spans from **initial device modeling** to **custom DT service development**, addressing the gap between static Building Information Modeling (BIM) representations and dynamic DT requirements. Key phases include:
  1. **Device Modeling and Configuration**: Building Control Engineers define sensors, actuators, and controllers within the BIM environment, enriching static BIM models with Building Control System (BCS) components to create a unified semantic foundation.
  2. **Model Transformation and Artifact Generation**: Leveraging **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles**, the framework extracts structured representations from enriched BIM models and **automatically generates deployable DT artifacts** (e.g., runtime configurations, service definitions) with minimal manual intervention.
  3. **DT Deployment**: The generated artifacts are deployed to synchronize the physical building and its digital twin, enabling real-time operational capabilities.
  4. **Runtime Operation and Monitoring**: Facility Managers interact with the DT to monitor system states, visualize data, and control physical infrastructure via immersive interfaces (e.g., interactive visualization tools).
  5. **Custom Service Integration and Evolution**: DT Engineers extend functionality by developing and integrating **custom services and plugins** through standardized extension points, allowing the DT to adapt to evolving operational requirements. The framework supports **modular architecture** with predefined interfaces for seamless integration.
  Transition management between phases is governed by **automated workflows** tied to MDE transformations, ensuring consistency and traceability. Lifecycle governance is implicit in the framework’s adherence to **stakeholder-specific use cases** (e.g., Building Control Engineers for modeling, Facility Managers for operations, DT Engineers for extensions), as outlined in Table I and Table II of the document."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system prioritizes **model fidelity** by leveraging **BIM’s semantically rich, object-based 3D models** to create a geometrically and semantically accurate representation of the physical building. The framework ensures **consistent runtime artifacts** through automated generation processes, minimizing manual intervention. Validity is quantified using the **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric**, which measures how well the resulting DT reflects its physical counterpart. While specific validation methods (e.g., runtime synchronization checks) are not detailed, the system’s reliance on **real-time data exchange** and **automated artifact generation** inherently supports **quality assurance** by maintaining alignment between the digital and physical domains."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT system’s **technical network infrastructure** is designed for **real-time, low-latency communication** between distributed components, leveraging a **modular architecture** with standardized extension points. While specific protocols (e.g., OPC UA, REST APIs) are not detailed, the framework supports **interoperability across heterogeneous systems** (e.g., BIM tools, IoT sensors, legacy building control systems). **Network topology** is implied to be **hybrid**, accommodating edge deployments (e.g., local sensor gateways) and centralized cloud/on-premise hubs. **Bandwidth and latency requirements** are critical for real-time synchronization, though exact thresholds are unspecified. **Reliability and redundancy** are addressed via the framework’s **runtime configuration management**, enabling dynamic adjustments without full redeployment. **Security protocols** are **not explicitly mentioned** in the provided documents; the non-functional requirements (NF_X) focus on **interoperability, scalability, maintainability, and accessibility**, with no reference to encryption or access control mechanisms."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **dt_hosting_deployment** infrastructure supports **flexible, scalable, and adaptable** deployment models, with the following technical specifications:
  - **Hosting Platforms**: Supports **cloud, edge, and on-premise** deployment (inferred from the framework’s modularity and NF_X scalability requirements).
  - **Computational and Storage Requirements**: Designed to handle **scalability from individual components to entire infrastructure systems**, with low-overhead updates (per NF_X maintainability). Exact resource allocations (e.g., CPU, memory) are unspecified.
  - **Scalability Mechanisms**: Leverages the **modular architecture** and **standardized extension points** to dynamically integrate new services or scale components without full redeployment. Runtime configuration management enables on-the-fly adjustments.
  - **Deployment Models Supported**: Includes **BIM-driven deployment** (via *Building Control Engineers*) and **operational DT deployment** (for *Facility Managers*), with a lifecycle managed by the DT Framework.
  - **Availability/Reliability Assurance**: Ensured through **runtime adaptability** and **low-overhead updates**, minimizing downtime. Redundancy and failover mechanisms are implied but not explicitly detailed."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system prioritizes **interoperability across heterogeneous systems** through a **modular, service-oriented architecture** with **standardized extension points**, enabling seamless integration with external tools and platforms. Key integration aspects include:
   - **Data exchange protocols**: Leverages **Eclipse Hono and Ditto** as the underlying layer for **real-time, bidirectional data synchronization** between physical assets and digital twins.
   - **Dependencies**: Explicit reliance on **BIM tools** (e.g., for initial device modeling and deployment) and **operational systems** (e.g., for real-time monitoring inputs).
   - **Integration patterns**: Supports **modular service integration**, allowing third-party analytics or control services to extend the DT’s capabilities via the framework’s extension points.
   - **Interoperability standards**: Aligned with non-functional requirements (**NF_X**) to ensure compatibility with **diverse AECO domain systems**, though specific protocols (e.g., OPC UA, IFC) or enterprise systems (e.g., ERP, CMMS) are not explicitly mentioned. The framework’s design emphasizes **low-overhead updates** and **accessibility** for users with varying technical expertise, implying broad horizontal scalability."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The framework adheres to **AECO-specific systems and standards** (e.g., BIM) as its foundational interoperability standard, addressing the gap between static BIM representations and dynamic DT requirements. **Device modeling** and **control logic definition** are standardized within the BIM environment, ensuring semantic consistency for subsequent transformations. The **modular architecture** enforces **standardized extension points** for integrating custom services, promoting interoperability with third-party tools and analytics. While the document does not explicitly cite **IFC (Industry Foundation Classes)** or **COBie**, the reliance on BIM implies compliance with **ISO 19650** (BIM standards) and **buildingSMART** frameworks. Compliance is further ensured through **automated model transformations** that map enriched BIM semantics to deployable DT artifacts, adhering to **model-driven engineering (MDE) principles** for governance and validation. No explicit certification requirements or additional industry standards (e.g., OPC UA, MTConnect) are mentioned."
]
